channel.title: HSJ - Latest news
channel.description: Custom RSS feed for HSJ latest news

item.title: Online hospital risks ‘leaving behind’ mental health, top CEO warns
item.description: Mental health is at risk of being “left behind” by the first online NHS hospital, a top mental health chief executive has said.

item.title: Trusts lose ‘red’ ratings after curbing deficits
item.description: NHS England has promoted two hospital trusts from its worst “capability” score, after they improved their financial performance at the end of 2025-26.

item.title: ‘Anti-Muslim hostility’ to be targeted by NHS
item.description: The NHS will strengthen efforts to combat “anti-Muslim hostility” after the government’s decision to commission a review focused on antisemitism caused unease.

item.title: Fresh concerns over safety and quality at maternity scandal trust
item.description: NHS England took action at a maternity scandal trust over “immediate risks” to women and babies, HSJ can reveal.

item.title: CEO admits underestimating requirements of deaths inquiry
item.description: A trust has been slow to provide records to an inquiry examining more than 2,000 deaths, because it underestimated the resources needed, its former CEO has admitted.

item.title: Consultants vote for industrial action
item.description: Medical consultants have voted in favour of industrial action over pay and working conditions, the British Medical Association has announced.

item.title: Exclusive: First complaints made over clinician use of AI
item.description: The first complaints about the alleged inappropriate use of AI by clinicians have been received by professional regulators, HSJ can reveal.

item.title: New GP leader seeks ‘collaborative approach’
item.description: The British Medical Association’s general practitioners committee for England has chosen a new chair after only one candidate put their name forward.

item.title: Trust appoints substantive CEO five weeks after hiring interim
item.description: A mental health trust has once again recruited its chief executive from its neighbouring provider.

item.title: NHSE orders ‘stocktake’ of patient communications
item.description: Trusts will be assessed on how they communicate with their patients as part of a “national stocktake” designed to ensure the NHS delivers “five-star customer service”, NHS England has announced.

item.title: Ministers remove adviser from national taskforce over ‘conflict of interest’
item.description: A medical adviser to the national maternity and neonatal taskforce has been asked to leave by ministers after they recognised “the depth of family concern” about his appointment.

item.title: NHSE makes three appointments to top team
item.description: NHS England has made three senior appointments as it readies itself for absorption by the Department of Health and Social Care, HSJ can reveal.

item.title: League tables ‘obscuring’ trust performance
item.description: Government’s trust league tables are “actively obscuring” patients’ understanding of their local services and should be scrapped, a think tank has recommended.

item.title: ‘Failure to deliver reforms has cost NHS billions’, claims ex-DHSC adviser
item.description: NHS England’s failure to deliver recommended reforms to pathology services has cost the service billions of pounds in lost savings over the last decade, a senior peer who previously reviewed the sector has claimed.

item.title: Watchdog launches investigation into NHSE claims of FDP effectiveness
item.description: The UK statistics watchdog has confirmed it is “reviewing” NHS England’s use of figures to promote the federated data platform, HSJ can reveal.

item.title: Capital approvals still ‘very, very elongated’, says CFO
item.description: Approval processes for NHS capital spending remain “very, very elongated” – despite promised improvements – an experienced chief finance officer has told MPs.

item.title: Regional director appointed hospital CEO
item.description: A hospital trust has appointed a long-serving medical leader – who is currently regional medical director – as its chief executive.

item.title: Call for dedicated maternity IT funding
item.description: The Royal College of Midwives has called for ring-fenced funding for dedicated, interoperable IT systems in maternity and neonatal care following the findings of the Amos Review.

item.title: Performance dips after trusts earn bonus cash
item.description: Most trusts that earned capital funding for improved A&E waiting times in March saw their performance deteriorate in the months after, HSJ can reveal.

item.title: Government picks CQC chair without competition
item.description: The government has revealed its preferred candidate to be the next chair of the Care Quality Commission, as a cross-bench peer who has overseen major national healthcare reviews.
